name: Can We Trust LLM's Logic? Quantifying Uncertainty, Coherence, and Robustness via a Graph-Based Framework

four_principles_filter.去粗取精: 研讨厅按 intake_score≥0.6 + 子系统匹配入库
four_principles_filter.去伪存真: 代码级落地/规划态已在决策中标注
four_principles_filter.由表及里: 研讨厅驱动正式入库固化

digest_notes: LLMs can be prone to flawed and unfaithful reasoning that decoding strategies like Self-Consistency fail to detect. We introduce GRAPHEVAL, a graph-based reasoning framework that re-frames uncertainty quantification as a holistic reasoning fidelity problem. We propose Graph Reasoning Coherence Score (GRCS), that quantifies semantic-structural consensus and captures pathological mode collapse. GRCS is the only metric consistently negatively correlated with reasoning faithfulness across models.
